name: Don Alias
abstract: Charles "Don" Alias (ah-LIE-ahs; December 25, 1939 – March 28, 2006) was an American jazz percussionist. Alias was best known for playing congas and other hand drums.

interviews.title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

works.t: Black is the Color of My True Love's Hair (Appalachian)

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Salutes the Jazz Masters: Verve Records at 50

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: So I called Victor and got him! And then he would say, “for the conga player I don’t wanna work with...” whoever it was I got him. “So who do you want?” And both Victor and Steve say “Don Alias!” “No problem!” I called Don Alias... I did that myself with great enthusiasm. And Don at that time was in
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Don Alias was a great musical constituent of mine... he worked with me... Then we did Oneness, formed the Oneness group which had Don Alias... and myself on the drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Rick Montalbano
about_quotes.evidence: especially becoming friends with some of the people in the band — Don Alias in particular... He and I played every day.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rick Montalbano interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 12, 2011

about_quotes.speaker: Chazz Frichtel
about_quotes.evidence: Don and I became fast friends, I think mostly because I knew Ricky and Carmen.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chazz Frichtel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 13, 2016

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Don Alias had something to say about the session that was kind of similar. I assume that you were sort of in a circle around miles logistically? Is that how that worked? [INTERVIEWEE] Not around Miles, no.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Brunious
about_quotes.evidence: Vladimir had a great Latin band, which he had like Al McKibbon and Don Alias and Bobby Rodriguez, and all these guys were in his band, Manny Duran.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Brunious interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]
